title: ephedra fragilis subsp. cossonii
intro: Ephedra fragilis subsp. cossonii, also known as the dwarf joint pine or jointed stem ephedra, is a small shrub-like plant belonging to the Ephedraceae family. It has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, particularly the presence of ephedrine and pseudoephedrine, which have been shown to have decongestant and bronchodilator effects. The health benefits of Ephedra fragilis subsp. cossonii include relief from respiratory issues such as asthma and bronchitis, as well as improved circulation and increased energy levels. The plant can be prepared as a tea, powder, or extract, and is often used in herbal remedies to enhance athletic performance and alleviate symptoms of fatigue and respiratory distress.
